_comment_identity: Identifies the target repo. repo_name is the local directory name.
_comment_description: Short description used in system prompts after 'The codebase is ...'
repo_description: a full-stack SDR 4G UE, eNodeB, and EPC implementation
_comment_system_prompt_context: Longer paragraph injected into agent system prompts describing what the repo contains, its language, build system, etc. Written as plain text (no markup).
system_prompt_context: The repo implements a software-defined radio (SDR) suite for 4G LTE networks, including user equipment (UE), evolved NodeB (eNodeB), and evolved packet core (EPC) components. The primary language is C++, with some C code. It uses CMake as the build system.
_comment_scan_dirs: Top-level directories to scan for function extraction.
_comment_subsystem_description: Human-readable subsystem mapping used in training system prompts.
subsystem_description: core library (lib), eNodeB application (srsenb), EPC implementation (srsepc), UE application (srsue), gNB application (srsgnb)
_comment_docker: Docker image prefix and container-internal path where the repo is mounted.
_comment_language: Primary language of the repo: c_cpp or go. Determines which language bug prompts to use.
_comment_domain: Domain for domain-specific bug prompts (e.g. telecom_5g). Empty string for generic repos.
_comment_build_note: Warning shown to agents about build limitations. Set to empty string if full builds work.
_comment_functions_file: Path to the extracted functions JSONL, relative to project root.
_comment_bug_prompts_file: Path to the assembled bug prompts JSON (language + domain). Generated by populate_repo_config.py.
_comment_fallback_demo_pr: Placeholder PR used when no demo_prs/ directory is found. Should be a realistic example from this repo.
fallback_demo_pr:
## Title: Fix buffer overflow in RRC connection reconfiguration message handling

### Summary
This PR fixes a buffer overflow vulnerability in the UE's RRC message processing code that could be triggered by a malformed connection reconfiguration message from the eNodeB. The issue occurred in the `rrc_connection_reconf_proc::parse_meas_config()` function where measurement object IDs were used as array indices without proper bounds checking. A malicious or buggy eNodeB could send a measurement configuration with an object ID larger than the allocated array size, causing memory corruption and potential remote code execution.

The fix adds proper bounds checking before using the measurement object ID as an array index and returns an error code when invalid IDs are detected. Additionally, the array size has been increased to accommodate the full range of valid measurement object IDs as defined in 3GPP TS 36.331.

### Changes
- `srsue/hdr/stack/rrc/rrc_connection_reconf_proc.cc`: Added bounds checking for measurement object IDs before array access and increased measurement object array size from 32 to 64 elements
- `srsue/hdr/stack/rrc/rrc_connection_reconf_proc.h`: Updated measurement object array size constant from 32 to 64

### Testing
- Added unit test case in `test/rrc/rrc_meas_test.cc` that sends malformed RRC connection reconfiguration messages with out-of-bounds measurement object IDs
- Verified fix with AddressSanitizer builds to confirm buffer overflow is prevented
- Tested with srsUE connecting to both valid and malicious eNodeB implementations to ensure proper error handling
